title: How Ismael Kone suffered serious injury as Canada routed Qatar behind Jonathan David hat-trick
original_text: Ismael Kone suffered a serious injury as Canada recorded the first men’s World Cup win in their history.That injury overshadowed an otherwise positive match for the co-hosts who ran out 6-0 victors in Group B. As he was leaving the pitch on a stretcher, Kone put his thumb up to the crowd inside BC Place.Jonathan David was the star for Canada, scoring his first World Cup hat-trick to go level with Lionel Messi in the race for the Golden Boot. Cyle Larin and Nathan Saliba also scored, while the co-hosts' fifth goal went down as a Mohamed Manai own goal.Join the Discuss tab or email us: live@theathletic.
_note: 全文走 summary 块A(LLM三判后只增不改);processed=成熟仓库累积,raw=本次收集过程。
